channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitics news headlines curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: China Confirms 200 Boeing Jet Purchase After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: China announced it will buy 200 Boeing aircraft and seek an extension of the US-China tariff truce through November, signalling a stabilization in relations between the world's two largest economies following last week's Beijing summit.

item.title: EU Strikes Deal to Finalize US Trade Agreement, Avert Tariffs
item.description: The European Union reached provisional agreement on legislation to remove import duties on US goods, a key part of a trade deal with Washington that would prevent higher US tariffs on EU products.

item.title: Trump Approval Drops to 35% as Republican Support Softens
item.description: A new Reuters/Ipsos poll shows President Donald Trump's approval rating falling to 35%, with softening support among Republicans as the administration navigates the Iran crisis and domestic challenges.

item.title: Ukraine Reinforces Northern Border Over Russian Offensive Plans
item.description: Ukraine will send reinforcements to its northern regions and step up diplomatic pressure on Belarus, citing intelligence that Russia plans a new offensive toward Kyiv from the north.

item.title: Fed Minutes Reveal Deep Divisions Over Rate Policy
item.description: The Federal Reserve's latest meeting minutes reveal significant disagreement among policymakers on interest rate direction and inflation severity, exposing the most divided Fed in a generation.

item.title: France Summons Israeli Envoy Over Ben-Gvir's Gaza Flotilla Video
item.description: Ireland's foreign minister expressed shock at footage showing Gaza flotilla activists being shot, while France summoned Israel's ambassador over National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir's comments about the incident.

item.title: Israel Should Treat Turkey as Enemy State, Says Culture Minister
item.description: Israeli Culture Minister Miki Zohar said Israel should treat Turkey as an enemy state amid escalating tensions between the two countries over the Gaza conflict and regional posture.

item.title: Baltic Lawmakers Press EU for Firm Support Amid Moscow Threats
item.description: Baltic state legislators are pushing the European Union for stronger support and clearer defense commitments as Russia threatens military strikes against countries hosting Western missiles.

item.title: UK Eases Some Russian Oil Sanctions as Fuel Prices Soar
item.description: The United Kingdom has eased some sanctions on Russian oil imports as domestic fuel prices continue to surge, prompting criticism over the government's handling of energy policy.

item.title: IDF Brigade Commander Seriously Wounded in South Lebanon Drone Strike
item.description: The commander of the IDF's 401st Brigade was seriously wounded in a drone strike in south Lebanon, amid ongoing cross-border clashes between Israeli forces and Hezbollah.

item.title: Lithuania on Alert After Suspected Russian Drone Incursion
item.description: Lithuania grounded flights and evacuated top leaders in Vilnius on Wednesday after a suspected Russian drone entered its airspace, with President Ursula von der Leyen vowing Europe will respond to such threats.

item.title: Rubio Visits Cuba, Offers Aid While Blaming Communist Leadership
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio visited Cuba and offered humanitarian aid while blaming the communist leadership for the country's economic crisis, in a rare high-level US diplomatic trip to the island.

item.title: Tankers Exit Hormuz as Trump and Vance Talk Up Iran Deal Prospects
item.description: Tankers departed the Strait of Hormuz as President Trump and Vice President Vance spoke optimistically about negotiations with Iran, saying talks are in final stages while warning of consequences if no deal is reached.

item.title: Rubio to Visit Sweden for NATO Meeting Before India Trip
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will visit Sweden for a NATO meeting before traveling to India, as the Trump administration continues its diplomatic outreach to key allies amid global tensions.

item.title: UK Inflation Slows to 2.8% in April
item.description: British inflation slowed to 2.8% in April, bringing some relief to consumers but remaining above the Bank of England's 2% target as the economy adjusts to post-Brexit realities.

item.title: Commerzbank Workers Protest Unicredit Takeover Attempt
item.description: Employees of Commerzbank demonstrated against Unicredit's attempted takeover, raising concerns about job security and the future of Germany's banking sector.

item.title: Kremlin Condemns Lithuanian Minister's Comments on Attacking Kaliningrad
item.description: The Kremlin said a Lithuanian minister's comments about attacking the Russian exclave of Kaliningrad verge on unacceptable, as tensions between Russia and Baltic states continue to escalate.

item.title: Qatar Airways Posts $1.9 Billion Full-Year Net Profit
item.description: Qatar Airways Group reported a $1.9 billion full-year net profit, showing strong recovery in global aviation demand despite ongoing regional tensions affecting Middle Eastern carriers.

item.title: Somaliland to Open Embassy in Jerusalem, Israel to Reciprocate
item.description: Somaliland announced it will open an embassy in Jerusalem, with Israel planning to reciprocate, in a move that signals expanding diplomatic ties for Israel in the Horn of Africa region.

item.title: US Senate Passes Symbolic War Powers Resolution as Trump Vows Quick End
item.description: The US Senate passed a symbolic war powers resolution regarding the Iran conflict, as President Trump vowed a "very quick end" to the military engagement while negotiations continue.

item.title: Handful of Tankers Still Crossing Strait of Hormuz Amid Tensions
item.description: A small number of tankers continue to navigate the Strait of Hormuz despite heightened tensions in the region, as energy markets watch for signs of disruption to critical oil shipping lanes.

item.title: Hormuz Blockage Forging New Overland Energy Map for Middle East
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz is reshaping Middle Eastern energy logistics, with countries developing new overland routes and infrastructure to bypass the critical waterway.

item.title: IRGC Threatens Attacks Beyond Region If US or Israel Strike Again
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard warned that any new US or Israeli attacks will be met with retaliation "in places you cannot even imagine," as diplomatic efforts to end the conflict continue.

item.title: Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin Meet in Beijing Summit
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin met in Beijing days after Trump's visit, discussing trade, energy cooperation, and global security amid ongoing geopolitical tensions.
